papers.title: A Calibrated Class-Weighted Ensemble Framework for Relationship Classification in SNOMED CT with Domain-Specific BERT Models
papers.authors: T M Rubaith Bashar, Mengjia Xu and James Geller

papers.title: Cluster-Guided Self-Supervised Learning with Triplet Refinement for Longitudinal Heart Rate Time Series
papers.authors: Hyungtaek Kwon, Isac Park, Wenchen Li, Elinor Schoenfeld and Fan Ye

keynote-anita.title: From Data to Decisions: Building Learning Health Systems in Hospitals
keynote-anita.speaker: Anita Burgun, MD, PhD, FIAHSI
keynote-anita.affiliation: Professor of Medical Informatics, Université Paris Cité; Director of Department of Medical Informatics, Georges Pompidou European Hospital and Necker Children’s Hospital
keynote-anita.bio: Prof. Anita Burgun is a full professor of biomedical informatics and statistics at Université Paris Cité. She leads the Departments of Medical Informatics at Georges Pompidou European Hospital and Necker Children’s Hospital. Her research focuses on clinical and translational biomedical informatics, large-scale health data integration, and data-driven approaches for precision medicine.

keynote-hoifung.title: Towards Virtual Patient: AI for Accelerating Medical Discovery
keynote-hoifung.speaker: Hoifung Poon, PhD
keynote-hoifung.abstract: This keynote explores how multimodal generative AI can learn the language of patients from longitudinal clinical, imaging, and multi-omics data to create virtual patient world models. These digital twins can synthesize population-scale real-world evidence and help accelerate medical discovery through AI-powered virtual clinical trials.

keynote-li.title: Harnessing AI and Informatics to Advance Dementia Research and Aging Care
keynote-li.speaker: Li Shen, PhD, FAIMBE, FACMI, FAMIA
keynote-li.affiliation: Professor of Informatics, Radiology and CIS, University of Pennsylvania, USA
keynote-li.abstract: This keynote highlights recent progress in integrating genetics, multi-omics, imaging, and clinical data to support dementia research and aging care. It also examines the role of trustworthy multimodal and generative AI in diagnosis, risk stratification, disease staging, and support for patients and caregivers.

industry-panel-1.title: Panel 1: Policy Session
summary: This block combines the Industry Track policy keynote and the policy panel on governing AI and health informatics in the age of intelligent care.
It covers AI accountability in clinical decision support, data privacy, health equity, and the governance of real-world data in healthcare and regulation.
participants: Keynote: Ram D. Sriram, PhD — Evaluating Large Language Models for Clinical Decision Support
Senator Eric Lucero, Minnesota Senate Representative
Jesse Isaacman-Beck, PhD, U.S. Department of Health & Human Services
Tyler Winkelman, MD, MSc, HHRI (MN HER Consortium)

industry-panel-2.title: Panel 2: The GenAI Data Chasm
summary: This panel examines the gap between having healthcare data and having data that is trustworthy, representative, and fit for a medical-grade AI ecosystem.
The discussion focuses on whether the field is building a human-aligned unified data ecosystem or creating deeper and more intelligent silos.

industry-panel-3.title: Panel 3: AI Enablement in Practice
summary: This panel explores practical strategies, enablers, and pitfalls in moving AI from research to real-world clinical deployment.
Topics include workflow integration, lifecycle management, regulation, bias mitigation, and building trust with clinicians and patients.

industry-keynote-ben.title: From Algorithm to Adoption: Evolving BeatLogic into Trusted Real-World AI for Cardiac Monitoring
industry-keynote-ben.speaker: Ben Teplitzky, PhD
industry-keynote-ben.affiliation: AI/ML Research Fellow, Patient Management, Boston Scientific, USA
industry-keynote-ben.abstract: Using BeatLogic as a case study, this keynote examines what it takes to move from an impressive algorithm to a trusted operational AI capability in ambulatory cardiac monitoring, including dataset design, annotation quality, validation strategy, workflow integration, and human oversight.

industry-networking.title: Professional Networking and Social Hour
summary: A networking-focused session for follow-up conversations across industry, academia, and healthcare delivery.
The current planning notes also include a speed-networking component in this block.
